name: tidy-why-complex
title: Complexity Inventory
description: Inventory essential versus accidental complexity in a bounded scope before simplification. Use when the immediate job is to identify complexity sources, not to plan the entire simplification yet.
purpose: Map complexity sources and classify them as essential or accidental before simplification work.
when_to_use: Need a bounded complexity inventory before simplification.
Need to know whether complexity comes from domain rules or accidental structure.
Need to identify which complexity clusters are worth removing first.
do_not_use: Need a full simplification plan already.
The target scope is not bounded.

structured_outputs.name: ESSENTIAL_COMPLEXITY
structured_outputs.description: Complexity that is inherent to the domain.

structured_outputs.name: ACCIDENTAL_COMPLEXITY
structured_outputs.description: Complexity created by incidental structure or naming.

neutrality_rules: Do not label domain-required complexity as accidental without evidence.
Separate pain points from actual simplification candidates.
Keep the inventory descriptive before recommending changes.
default_program: [stages: preflight>detect>analyze>review>handoff>audit | scope: repo|diff|paths(glob,...) | policy: evidence,deterministic-output | lens: hickey-carmack | output: md(contract=v1)]
lens_rationale: Complexity inventory should separate essential structure from accidental abstraction and hidden state.
